findings.finding: unregister() in settings_merge.py operates exclusively on sandbox.filesystem.allowWrite and never touches additionalDirectories. The migration predicate in the spec requires cleaning both arrays, but even if unregister() were wired into --update, the additionalDirectories stale entry would survive.
findings.evidence_quote: removes orphaned entries: any value containing cortex-worktrees from sandbox.filesystem.allowWrite and additionalDirectories

findings.finding: R5's allow_paths fix in seatbelt_probe.py must simultaneously cover TMPDIR (for probe result/output files written at lines 156-157) AND <repo>/.claude/worktrees/ (for branch (c) worktree creation inside the spawned claude session), but the spec's acceptance criterion only checks for a substring match in the allow_paths construction and does not verify that TMPDIR coverage is preserved.
findings.evidence_quote: The probe's allow_paths no longer relies solely on TMPDIR; grep -c 'claude/worktrees|repo_root|project_root' cortex_command/overnight/seatbelt_probe.py >= 1 in the allow_paths construction

findings.finding: test_worktree_seatbelt.py is not listed in R8 update scope or R10 test sweep. After the revert, the test module docstring explicitly claims to validate TMPDIR/cortex-worktrees/<feature> semantics (the path being reverted away from), becoming factually inverted.
findings.evidence_quote: Update tests/test_worktree.py: revert branch-(c) assertions to expect .claude/worktrees/; delete branch-(b) sentinel tests; add a new integration test...
